summary: This notebook demonstrates a post-processing ensemble pipeline that combines predictions from five independently trained models to generate a final competition submission. It orchestrates external inference scripts, loads the resulting CSV files, aligns them by sample ID, and computes a weighted average of the target predictions. The author notes that this approach achieves a competitive score of approximately 0.7 while maintaining a manageable inference runtime of roughly 7.45 hours. Readers should take away how straightforward weighted ensembling can effectively aggregate diverse model outputs without requiring additional training or complex post-processing. The notebook also serves as a practical template for managing multi-model inference and submission generation in Kaggle competitions.
competition_flow: Loads five pre-generated model predictions from CSV files, merges them by sample ID, applies a weighted average, and exports the final ensemble submission.
data_reading: Reads five submission CSV files (submission1.csv to submission5.csv) using pandas, each containing a sample_id column and a target column.
data_processing: Renames target columns to target_1 through target_5, merges all DataFrames on sample_id, and calculates a weighted average of the predictions.
ensembling: Combines five model predictions using a weighted average with weights [0.19, 0.27, 0.22, 0.22, 0.10] applied to the respective target columns before saving the final submission.
insights: Simple weighted averaging of diverse model predictions can effectively boost competition scores without additional training.
External inference scripts can be orchestrated directly in a notebook to manage complex or time-consuming prediction pipelines.
Proper column renaming and merging by a common identifier is crucial for aligning heterogeneous model outputs.
